goal: Provide a governed architecture opinion on the current chassis control framework, especially the meaning and usefulness of open-loop versus closed-loop control and the surrounding command/protocol structure.
deliverable: A concise but concrete architecture assessment with recommended direction, preserved as vibe artifacts.
constraints: Do not overstate field performance conclusions without new testing.
Base the opinion on the current code paths rather than abstract textbook control advice.
Keep the recommendations actionable for future firmware refactoring.
acceptance_criteria: Explain what the current framework is actually doing.
Identify the main architectural mismatches or ambiguities.
Recommend a cleaner target control architecture for this robot.
product_acceptance_criteria: The user can decide whether to keep, simplify, or redesign the current open-loop/closed-loop split.
The opinion clearly distinguishes command modes, control modes, and communication modes.
manual_spot_checks: Compare the stated architecture assessment against the current source files.
Use the recommendation later as a baseline for mode/protocol cleanup work.
completion_language_policy: Do not claim the framework is production-ready; only assess its current structure and likely next direction.
delivery_truth_contract: Only claim properties that are visible in the current code.
inferred_assumptions: The user wants architectural judgment before further modification.
The present code has enough structure to support a clear recommendation without requiring new hardware data.
